basic_details.job_title: CGPSC Sahayak Jila Lok Abhiyojan Adhikari Recruitment 2026 - Apply Online for 15 Posts
basic_details.short_information: The Chhattisgarh Public Service Commission (CGPSC) has released Advertisement No. 03/2026/Exam dated 17 June 2026 to fill 15 posts of Sahayak Jila Lok Abhiyojan Adhikari (Assistant District Public Prosecution Officer) in the Home Department, Government of Chhattisgarh. The online application window opens on 22 June 2026 (12:00 PM) and closes on 21 July 2026 (11:59 PM), with selection through a written exam and interview, and pay at Level-9 (Rs. 38,100–1,20,400) of the pay matrix.

application_fee.details: Candidates asking how much the CGPSC ADPPO 2026 application fee costs can note that it varies by category and domicile status, with an additional portal charge applicable to everyone. Local residents of Chhattisgarh belonging to SC, ST, or OBC (non-creamy layer), as well as local disabled candidates, pay Rs. 300, while all other candidates, including those from outside Chhattisgarh, pay Rs. 400; in both cases, a portal fee plus GST is charged additionally to all applicants. | Candidates should also note that local residents of Chhattisgarh who actually appear in the written exam or interview will get their examination fee refunded to the bank account entered during the application process, though the portal fee, GST, and any correction charges are never refunded.

age_limit.details: The standard age limit is minimum 21 years and maximum 30 years as on 1 January 2026 , based on the date of birth recorded in the Matriculation or equivalent certificate. However, considering the interest of educated unemployed youth, the State Government grants local residents of Chhattisgarh a 5-year relaxation on the special upper limit of 35 years, effectively allowing local candidates to apply up to 40 years of age (this relaxation period currently runs from 1 January 2024 to 31 December 2028). | On top of this, additional relaxations in the upper age limit are available as follows: | SC/ST/OBC (non-creamy layer) of Chhattisgarh: 5 years relaxation on the higher age limit. Government/corporation employees of Chhattisgarh (regular, temporary, or work-charge): upper age limit capped at 38 years. Ex-servicemen of Chhattisgarh: relaxation equal to defence service period, but the resulting age must not exceed the higher limit by more than 3 years. Retrenched government servants: relaxation for past temporary service (up to 7 years), but resulting age must not exceed the higher limit by more than 3 years. Widowed, divorced, or separated women of Chhattisgarh: 5 years relaxation. Contractual employees of Chhattisgarh: relaxation equal to years of contractual service, capped at age 38. Persons with disabilities: 5 years relaxation. Shiksha Karmi / Panchayat Karmi: relaxation equal to years of service, capped at age 45. | Candidates should note that all relaxations combined cannot push the maximum age limit beyond 45 years , and reservation/age-relaxation benefits are available only to bonafide local/domicile residents of Chhattisgarh.

qualification.details: Candidates wondering about the qualification and age required for this post can note that CGPSC requires a Bachelor's degree in Law from a recognized university, along with an age between 21 and 30 years as on 1 January 2026, with category-specific relaxations available. Meeting these minimum criteria does not automatically guarantee a call for the exam or interview, since the Commission can further screen candidates based on these or higher qualifications. | Candidates must hold a Bachelor's Degree in Law (LLB) from a university recognized by the UGC. The post is classified as Gazetted, Second Class, and selected candidates are appointed on a 3-year probation period. Candidates must hold the required educational qualification certificate by the last date of online application; degrees awarded after the closing date will not be considered valid. | The standard age limit is minimum 21 years and maximum 30 years as on 1 January 2026, based on the date of birth recorded in the Matriculation or equivalent certificate. However, considering the interest of educated unemployed youth, the State Government grants local residents of Chhattisgarh a 5-year relaxation on the special upper limit of 35 years, effectively allowing local candidates to apply up to 40 years of age (this relaxation period currently runs from 1 January 2024 to 31 December 2028). | On top of this, additional relaxations in the upper age limit are available as follows: | SC/ST/OBC (non-creamy layer) of Chhattisgarh: 5 years relaxation on the higher age limit. Government/corporation employees of Chhattisgarh (regular, temporary, or work-charge): upper age limit capped at 38 years. Ex-servicemen of Chhattisgarh: relaxation equal to defence service period, but the resulting age must not exceed the higher limit by more than 3 years. Retrenched government servants: relaxation for past temporary service (up to 7 years), but resulting age must not exceed the higher limit by more than 3 years. Widowed, divorced, or separated women of Chhattisgarh: 5 years relaxation. Contractual employees of Chhattisgarh: relaxation equal to years of contractual service, capped at age 38. Persons with disabilities: 5 years relaxation. Shiksha Karmi / Panchayat Karmi: relaxation equal to years of service, capped at age 45. | Candidates should note that all relaxations combined cannot push the maximum age limit beyond 45 years, and reservation/age-relaxation benefits are available only to bonafide local/domicile residents of Chhattisgarh.

salary_details.details: Candidates asking about the salary for this post can note that the Sahayak Jila Lok Abhiyojan Adhikari post carries Pay Matrix Level-9, with a pay scale of Rs. 38,100 to Rs. 1,20,400, in addition to Dearness Allowance and other allowances as notified by the State Government from time to time. This pay scale applies uniformly to both the regular and carry-forward/backlog vacancies advertised under this post. | In-Hand Salary Calculator

selection_process: Stage Component Marks / Details
1 Written Exam (Objective Type) 150 questions, 3 hours, 300 marks total
1A Part 1 – General Knowledge 50 questions, 100 marks
1B Part 2 – Law (Major Acts + Minor Acts) 100 questions, 200 marks
2 Interview 30 marks
Final Merit List Combined exam + interview marks (330 total), category-wise
Candidates curious about how CGPSC shortlists the Assistant District Public Prosecution Officer can note that selection happens in two stages: a written exam followed by an interview
with the written exam carrying 300 marks and the interview carrying 30 marks
for a combined total of 330 marks. The written exam consists of a single objective-type paper of 150 questions to be completed in 3 hours
with negative marking for wrong answers.
The exam paper is bilingual (Hindi and English)
and the Commission may conduct it as a Computer Based Test (CBT) depending on the number of applicants. Negative marking is applied using the formula MO = M × R − (1/3) × M × W
where MO is marks obtained
M is marks per correct answer
R is the number of correct answers
and W is the number of wrong answers. To qualify

candidates must score a minimum of 33% in each paper for Unreserved category candidates and 23% for reserved category candidates; there is no minimum qualifying mark for the interview. Candidates called for interview will be approximately three times the number of vacancies
drawn only from those declared qualified in the written exam
and the written exam will be held at centres in Ambikapur
Bilaspur
Raipur
Durg
and Jagdalpur (subject to change based on the number of applicants).
how_to_apply: Candidates wondering how to submit their application can follow the numbered steps below, all of which take place online through the official CGPSC portal. Importantly, no certificates need to be uploaded at the time of online application, since document verification happens only at a later stage.
Visit the official CGPSC website at www.psc.cg.gov.in.

First-time applicants must complete "Candidate's Registration" by entering name, father's name, mother's name, gender, date of birth, mobile number, email ID, and ID proof number, then generate and verify the OTP sent to the registered mobile/email.

Capture a live photo using the mobile camera link sent after OTP verification - ensure the face is clear, both ears are visible, eyes are open, and the background is a single light colour, with the face covering about 80% of the frame.
Sign on a plain white sheet with a black ball-point pen, scan only the signature (not the blank background), and upload the signature as a JPG file (maximum 100 KB).
Click Save, then go to the dashboard and click "Apply" against the Sahayak Jila Lok Abhiyojan Adhikari post.
Carefully fill in all required details in the online application form.
Pay the application fee plus portal fee and GST through the available online payment modes (credit card, debit card, net banking, UPI, etc.).
Confirm that the Application Status shows "Submitted" and the Payment Status shows "Paid" on the receipt page.
Download and save the application receipt and payment receipt, since these will be required at later stages of the selection process.

content_sections.heading: CGPSC Sahayak Jila Lok Abhiyojan Adhikari Recruitment 2026 - Quick Summary

rows.information: Sahayak Jila Lok Abhiyojan Adhikari (Assistant District Public Prosecution Officer)

content_sections.heading: Important Dates for CGPSC ADPPO 2026
content_sections.text: Candidates tracking the CGPSC Assistant District Public Prosecution Officer 2026 timeline can refer to the dates below, all confirmed in the official notification. The online application process starts on 22 June 2026 at 12:00 PM noon and the last date to apply online and pay the fee is 21 July 2026 at 11:59 PM , after which a correction window opens before the probable exam date of 20 September 2026 .

content_sections.heading: Vacancy Breakdown for CGPSC ADPPO 2026
content_sections.text: Candidates checking the exact vacancy distribution can note that CGPSC has advertised a total of 15 posts , split into 10 regular vacancies and 5 carry-forward/backlog vacancies across Unreserved (UR), SC, ST, and OBC categories. Out of the regular Unreserved vacancies, 1 post is reserved for local women candidates of Chhattisgarh , and within the total vacancies, 5 posts are horizontally reserved for "One Arm" (OA) disabled local candidates of Chhattisgarh (3 under UR, 1 under SC, and 1 under ST).

content_sections.heading: Eligibility Criteria for CGPSC ADPPO 2026
content_sections.text: Candidates wondering about the qualification and age required for this post can note that CGPSC requires a Bachelor's degree in Law from a recognized university , along with an age between 21 and 30 years as on 1 January 2026 , with category-specific relaxations available. Meeting these minimum criteria does not automatically guarantee a call for the exam or interview, since the Commission can further screen candidates based on these or higher qualifications. Candidates must hold a Bachelor's Degree in Law (LLB) from a university recognized by the UGC. The post is classified as Gazetted, Second Class , and selected candidates are appointed on a 3-year probation period . Candidates must hold the required educational qualification certificate by the last date of online application ; degrees awarded after the closing date will not be considered valid. The standard age limit is minimum 21 years and maximum 30 years as on 1 January 2026 , based on the date of birth recorded in the Matriculation or equivalent certificate. However, considering the interest of educated unemployed youth, the State Government grants local residents of Chhattisgarh a 5-year relaxation on the special upper limit of 35 years, effectively allowing local candidates to apply up to 40 years of age (this relaxation period currently runs from 1 January 2024 to 31 December 2028). On top of this, additional relaxations in the upper age limit are available as follows: SC/ST/OBC (non-creamy layer) of Chhattisgarh: 5 years relaxation on the higher age limit. Government/corporation employees of Chhattisgarh (regular, temporary, or work-charge): upper age limit capped at 38 years. Ex-servicemen of Chhattisgarh: relaxation equal to defence service period, but the resulting age must not exceed the higher limit by more than 3 years. Retrenched government servants: relaxation for past temporary service (up to 7 years), but resulting age must not exceed the higher limit by more than 3 years. Widowed, divorced, or separated women of Chhattisgarh: 5 years relaxation. Contractual employees of Chhattisgarh: relaxation equal to years of contractual service, capped at age 38. Persons with disabilities: 5 years relaxation. Shiksha Karmi / Panchayat Karmi: relaxation equal to years of service, capped at age 45. Candidates should note that all relaxations combined cannot push the maximum age limit beyond 45 years , and reservation/age-relaxation benefits are available only to bonafide local/domicile residents of Chhattisgarh.

content_sections.heading: Application Fee for CGPSC ADPPO 2026
content_sections.text: Candidates asking how much the CGPSC ADPPO 2026 application fee costs can note that it varies by category and domicile status, with an additional portal charge applicable to everyone. Local residents of Chhattisgarh belonging to SC, ST, or OBC (non-creamy layer), as well as local disabled candidates, pay Rs. 300 , while all other candidates, including those from outside Chhattisgarh, pay Rs. 400 ; in both cases, a portal fee plus GST is charged additionally to all applicants . Candidates should also note that local residents of Chhattisgarh who actually appear in the written exam or interview will get their examination fee refunded to the bank account entered during the application process, though the portal fee, GST, and any correction charges are never refunded.

rows.category: Local CG residents – SC/ST/OBC (non-creamy layer) & local disabled candidates
All other categories & candidates from outside Chhattisgarh

content_sections.heading: Selection Process for CGPSC ADPPO 2026
content_sections.text: Candidates curious about how CGPSC shortlists the Assistant District Public Prosecution Officer can note that selection happens in two stages: a written exam followed by an interview , with the written exam carrying 300 marks and the interview carrying 30 marks , for a combined total of 330 marks . The written exam consists of a single objective-type paper of 150 questions to be completed in 3 hours, with negative marking for wrong answers. The exam paper is bilingual ( Hindi and English ), and the Commission may conduct it as a Computer Based Test (CBT) depending on the number of applicants. Negative marking is applied using the formula MO = M × R − (1/3) × M × W , where MO is marks obtained, M is marks per correct answer, R is the number of correct answers, and W is the number of wrong answers. To qualify, candidates must score a minimum of 33% in each paper for Unreserved category candidates and 23% for reserved category candidates ; there is no minimum qualifying mark for the interview . Candidates called for interview will be approximately three times the number of vacancies , drawn only from those declared qualified in the written exam, and the written exam will be held at centres in Ambikapur, Bilaspur, Raipur, Durg, and Jagdalpur (subject to change based on the number of applicants).

rows.marks_details: Combined exam + interview marks (330 total), category-wise

content_sections.heading: How to Apply for CGPSC ADPPO 2026
content_sections.text: Candidates wondering how to submit their application can follow the numbered steps below, all of which take place online through the official CGPSC portal. Importantly, no certificates need to be uploaded at the time of online application , since document verification happens only at a later stage. Visit the official CGPSC website at www.psc.cg.gov.in . First-time applicants must complete "Candidate's Registration" by entering name, father's name, mother's name, gender, date of birth, mobile number, email ID, and ID proof number, then generate and verify the OTP sent to the registered mobile/email. Capture a live photo using the mobile camera link sent after OTP verification - ensure the face is clear, both ears are visible, eyes are open, and the background is a single light colour, with the face covering about 80% of the frame. Sign on a plain white sheet with a black ball-point pen, scan only the signature (not the blank background), and upload the signature as a JPG file (maximum 100 KB) . Click Save , then go to the dashboard and click "Apply" against the Sahayak Jila Lok Abhiyojan Adhikari post. Carefully fill in all required details in the online application form. Pay the application fee plus portal fee and GST through the available online payment modes (credit card, debit card, net banking, UPI, etc.). Confirm that the Application Status shows "Submitted" and the Payment Status shows "Paid" on the receipt page. Download and save the application receipt and payment receipt , since these will be required at later stages of the selection process. Direct Link to Apply

content_sections.heading: Documents and Consent Requirements
content_sections.text: Candidates preparing for this recruitment should know that no documents or certificates are required to be attached with the online application itself ; verification happens only when a candidate is called for interview. At that stage, candidates must submit self-certified or gazetted-officer-certified copies of the Matriculation/Higher Secondary certificate for age proof, all semester/year mark sheets for the required qualification, the Bachelor's degree in Law certificate, and a valid caste certificate (if claiming SC/ST/OBC reservation benefits, with specific documentation needed for married SC/ST women and for OBC non-creamy-layer status, including an income certificate issued within the last 3 years). Candidates currently employed in government departments, PSUs, banks, or universities can apply online but should request a No Objection Certificate (NOC) from their employer to be sent directly to the Commission, and must carry proof of having requested it if called for interview, since failure to secure an NOC after selection can result in cancellation of appointment with no liability on the Commission.

content_sections.heading: Salary and Pay Scale for CGPSC ADPPO 2026
content_sections.text: Candidates asking about the salary for this post can note that the Sahayak Jila Lok Abhiyojan Adhikari post carries Pay Matrix Level-9, with a pay scale of Rs. 38,100 to Rs. 1,20,400 , in addition to Dearness Allowance and other allowances as notified by the State Government from time to time. This pay scale applies uniformly to both the regular and carry-forward/backlog vacancies advertised under this post. In-Hand Salary Calculator

content_sections.heading: Common Mistakes Candidates Should Avoid
content_sections.text: Many candidates risk rejection due to avoidable errors in the application process rather than a lack of qualification. Reviewing these common pitfalls before applying can help candidates avoid delays or disqualification later in the selection process. Uploading a poor-quality signature scan: The signature must be scanned cleanly from a plain white sheet using a black ball-point pen, cropped to show only the signature (not the surrounding blank space), and kept under 100 KB in JPG format. Taking the live photo with an unsuitable background: Photos taken with a cluttered background, sunglasses, or a cap can cause issues later, since the system requires a single light-coloured background with the face filling about 80% of the frame and both ears clearly visible. Assuming all fields can be corrected later: The free and paid correction windows (22–26 July 2026) allow changes only to gender, date of birth, category, domicile, disability, and ex-servicemen status - no other field, including qualification details, can be corrected at any stage, so candidates should verify every entry before final submission. Not requesting an NOC while in government service: Candidates already employed in government, PSU, bank, or university roles should apply for their employer's No Objection Certificate immediately after applying, since being called for interview without this proof can later block appointment even after selection. Switching mobile numbers or email IDs mid-process: Since all future communication, OTPs, and admit cards are linked to the mobile number and email entered during registration, changing them before the selection process concludes can cause candidates to miss important updates. Missing the exam answer-key objection window: Candidates who notice errors in questions or answer options during the exam must raise objections online within the specific time window announced by the Commission; objections raised later are not considered.

faq.question: Q1. What is the last date to apply for CGPSC Sahayak Jila Lok Abhiyojan Adhikari 2026?
faq.answer: The last date to submit the online application and pay the fee is 21 July 2026 at 11:59 PM.

faq.question: Q2. How many vacancies are there in this CGPSC recruitment?
faq.answer: There are 15 total vacancies - 10 regular and 5 carry-forward/backlog posts - distributed across UR, SC, ST, and OBC categories.

faq.question: Q3. What is the eligibility for CGPSC Assistant District Public Prosecution Officer?
faq.answer: Candidates need a Bachelor's Degree in Law from a recognized university and must be between 21 and 30 years of age as on 1 January 2026, with relaxations available for specific categories.

faq.question: Q4. What is the application fee for CGPSC ADPPO 2026?
faq.answer: The fee is Rs. 300 for local SC/ST/OBC (non-creamy layer) and local disabled candidates of Chhattisgarh, and Rs. 400 for all other candidates, plus a portal fee and GST for everyone.

faq.question: Q5. What is the selection process for this post?
faq.answer: Selection is through a written exam (300 marks) covering General Knowledge and Law, followed by an interview (30 marks), with the final merit list based on the combined 330 marks.

faq.question: Q6. What is the salary of Sahayak Jila Lok Abhiyojan Adhikari?
faq.answer: The post carries Pay Matrix Level-9 with a pay scale of Rs. 38,100 to Rs. 1,20,400, plus applicable allowances, and is a gazetted second-class post with a 3-year probation period.

faq.question: Q7. When is the CGPSC ADPPO 2026 exam likely to be held?
faq.answer: The probable exam date is 20 September 2026, as mentioned in the official notification.

faq.question: Q8. Can I correct mistakes in my CGPSC application after submission?
faq.answer: Yes, a free correction window runs from 22 to 24 July 2026, and a paid correction window (Rs. 500 fee) runs from 25 to 26 July 2026, but corrections are limited only to gender, date of birth, category, domicile, disability, and ex-servicemen status.

faq.question: Q9. Do I need to upload documents with the online application?
faq.answer: No, no certificates need to be uploaded at the application stage; documents are verified only when a candidate is shortlisted for the interview.

faq.question: Q10. What is the official website for CGPSC Sahayak Jila Lok Abhiyojan Adhikari 2026?
faq.answer: The official website is www.psc.cg.gov.in, where candidates can apply online and check all updates related to this recruitment.
